title: Introduction to Metal
description: Discover Metal - Apple's framework for GPU-accelerated graphics and compute.

sections.content:
## Introduction to Metal

Metal provides extremely low-overhead access to the GPU, enabling you to maximize the graphics and compute potential of your app.

### Why Metal?

- **Performance** - Near-direct GPU access
- **Parallelism** - Execute thousands of threads simultaneously
- **Flexibility** - Custom rendering pipelines
- **Compatibility** - Works across all Apple platforms

### Metal vs Other Graphics APIs

| Feature | Metal | OpenGL | Vulkan |
|---------|-------|--------|--------|
| Platform | Apple only | Cross-platform | Cross-platform |
| Learning curve | Moderate | Steep | Very steep |
| Performance | Excellent | Good | Excellent |

### Core Metal Concepts

1. **Device** - Represents the GPU
2. **Command Queue** - Manages command buffers
3. **Buffer** - Holds vertex/index data
4. **Texture** - Stores image data
5. **Shader** - GPU execution code

sections.title: Metal Setup Overview
sections.expectedOutput:
Metal enables GPU-accelerated rendering
Key components: Device, CommandQueue, Pipeline, Buffers
Shaders written in Metal Shading Language (MSL)
sections.solution:
// Metal setup pseudocode
print("Metal enables GPU-accelerated rendering")

sections.question: What language are Metal shaders written in?
sections.explanation: Metal shaders are written in Metal Shading Language (MSL), which is based on C++.
